name: World Music
description: From Beethoven to Beyoncé — how well do you know the world of music?
author: Game Night Crew

questions.question: Which member of The Beatles was known as the 'Quiet Beatle'?
questions.explanation: George Harrison was the quiet one — but his guitar work spoke volumes. He wrote 'Here Comes the Sun' and 'Something', so he definitely wasn't silent!

questions.question: What was Michael Jackson's famous nickname?
questions.explanation: Michael Jackson is the King of Pop — no debate. From 'Thriller' to the moonwalk, nobody did it like MJ.

questions.question: Which rapper is known for hits like 'Hotline Bling' and 'God's Plan'?
questions.explanation: Drake — the Toronto legend. 'Hotline Bling' had everyone doing that awkward dance. You know the one.

questions.question: Which band performed the iconic rock anthem 'Bohemian Rhapsody'?
questions.explanation: Queen gave us 'Bohemian Rhapsody' — six minutes of pure genius. Freddie Mercury was on another level, no cap.

questions.question: Taylor Swift's 2014 album '1989' marked her transition from which genre to pop?
questions.explanation: Taylor started in country music before going full pop with '1989'. Smart move — she basically conquered two genres.

questions.question: Which South Korean boy band became a global phenomenon with hits like 'Dynamite' and 'Butter'?
questions.explanation: BTS (Bangtan Sonyeondan) broke every record going. Their ARMY fanbase is basically its own nation at this point.

questions.question: What instrument does a DJ primarily use to mix music?
questions.explanation: Turntables are the DJ's bread and butter. From Grandmaster Flash to Black Coffee — the decks are where the magic happens!

questions.question: Beyoncé was the lead singer of which girl group before going solo?
questions.explanation: Destiny's Child gave us 'Say My Name' and 'Survivor' before Beyoncé went on to become Queen Bey. The rest is history!

questions.question: Which legendary rapper was fatally shot in Las Vegas in September 1996?
questions.explanation: Tupac was shot on September 7, 1996, and passed away six days later. Gone way too soon — 2Pac's influence on hip hop is still massive.

questions.question: Which Nigerian artist won the Grammy for Best Global Music Album in 2021 with 'Twice As Tall'?
questions.explanation: Burna Boy took home that Grammy and put Afrobeats on the global map even more. The African Giant indeed!

questions.question: Which song by Luis Fonsi and Daddy Yankee became the first YouTube video to reach 5 billion views?
questions.explanation: Despacito was EVERYWHERE in 2017. You couldn't escape it — at the braai, in the taxi, at the shops. It broke YouTube!

questions.question: How many strings does a standard guitar have?
questions.explanation: Six strings on a standard guitar. A bass guitar has 4 though — don't get confused at the music shop!

questions.question: What is the best-selling album of all time worldwide?
options: The Dark Side of the Moon — Pink Floyd
questions.explanation: Thriller sold over 70 million copies worldwide. MJ really said 'hold my glove' and made history.

questions.question: Which music award show hands out the famous golden gramophone trophy?
questions.explanation: The Grammy Awards give out that iconic golden gramophone. It's basically the Oscars of music — every artist wants one on the shelf.

questions.question: Kurt Cobain was the frontman of which grunge band?
questions.explanation: Nirvana and 'Smells Like Teen Spirit' basically invented the '90s grunge movement. Kurt Cobain was a generational talent.

questions.question: Which rapper's real name is Shawn Corey Carter?
questions.explanation: Jay-Z went from Marcy Projects to billionaire status. Shawn Carter built an empire — Roc Nation, Tidal, and married Beyoncé. Power couple goals!

questions.question: What does 'BPM' stand for in music production?
questions.explanation: BPM = Beats Per Minute. It's how you measure tempo. House music usually sits around 120-130 BPM — perfect for the dancefloor!

questions.question: Which classical composer became deaf later in life but continued to compose masterpieces?
questions.explanation: Beethoven started losing his hearing in his late 20s and was nearly totally deaf by 44 — but still composed his 9th Symphony. Absolute legend move.

questions.question: Fela Kuti is considered the pioneer of which music genre?
questions.explanation: Fela Kuti created Afrobeat (no 's') — blending jazz, funk, and West African rhythms. Modern 'Afrobeats' with an 's' is a different genre, but Fela inspired it all!

questions.question: Which Kendrick Lamar album won the Pulitzer Prize for Music in 2018?
questions.explanation: DAMN. won the Pulitzer — the first non-classical, non-jazz album to do so. Kendrick literally changed what's possible in hip hop.

questions.question: Which instrument family does the violin belong to?
questions.explanation: Violin is a string instrument — you play it with a bow (or pizzicato if you're feeling fancy). It's the soprano of the string family.

questions.question: Which Swedish DJ, born Tim Bergling, passed away in 2018 and was known for hits like 'Wake Me Up'?
questions.explanation: Avicii was one of EDM's biggest names. 'Wake Me Up' and 'Levels' were absolute bangers. His passing was a huge loss to music.

questions.question: In music notation, how many beats does a whole note (semibreve) receive in 4/4 time?
questions.explanation: A whole note gets 4 beats in 4/4 time. It fills up the entire bar — like ordering the full bunny chow, not the quarter.

questions.question: Which reggaeton artist released the hit 'Gasolina' in 2004, helping bring the genre to global attention?
questions.explanation: Daddy Yankee's 'Gasolina' was THE song that introduced reggaeton to the world. Every party from Durban to Dubai was playing it!

questions.question: Which artist has won the most Grammy Awards of all time (as of 2024)?
questions.explanation: Beyoncé holds the record with 32 Grammy wins as of 2024. Queen Bey stays collecting trophies like they're going out of fashion!

questions.question: What is the name of the Nigerian music genre that blends hip hop, dancehall, and West African rhythms, popularised by Wizkid and Burna Boy?
questions.explanation: Afrobeats (with the 's') is the modern genre from Nigeria and Ghana that's taken over the world. Different from Fela's Afrobeat — though the roots are connected!

questions.question: Led Zeppelin's 'Stairway to Heaven' is considered one of the greatest rock songs ever. How long is the original studio version?
questions.explanation: 'Stairway to Heaven' clocks in at about 8 minutes. It builds from acoustic to electric like a proper lekker crescendo. Worth every second!

questions.question: Put these iconic music decades in order, from earliest to latest, based on when each genre FIRST dominated mainstream charts:
questions.explanation: Motown dominated the 1960s, Disco ruled the late 1970s, Grunge broke through in the early 1990s, and EDM hit the mainstream in the 2010s. A proper timeline of vibes!

questions.question: Which Mozart opera tells the story of a young woman who is kidnapped from a harem?
questions.explanation: 'Die Entführung aus dem Serail' (The Abduction from the Seraglio) premiered in 1782. Mozart was only 26 and already cooking with gas!

questions.question: What was the first music video ever played on MTV when it launched on August 1, 1981?
options: 'Video Killed the Radio Star' — The Buggles
questions.explanation: 'Video Killed the Radio Star' by The Buggles — how fitting for the first ever MTV music video! The irony was chef's kiss.

questions.question: In music theory, what is the interval between C and G called?
questions.explanation: C to G is a perfect fifth — one of the most important intervals in music. It's the backbone of power chords in rock!

questions.question: Which South African DJ and producer is known globally and has played at major festivals like Coachella and Tomorrowland?
questions.explanation: Black Coffee is Mzansi's biggest DJ export. From Durban to the world stage — he even won a Grammy in 2022. Proudly South African!

questions.question: Which K-pop girl group released the hit 'DDU-DU DDU-DU' and became the highest-charting female K-pop act on the Billboard Hot 100?
questions.explanation: BLACKPINK in your area! Jisoo, Jennie, Rosé, and Lisa became global superstars. 'DDU-DU DDU-DU' was absolutely massive.

questions.question: Bach's 'The Well-Tempered Clavier' was written to demonstrate music in all 24 what?
questions.explanation: Bach wrote preludes and fugues in all 24 major and minor keys. It was basically a flex to show that the tuning system worked. Bach was that guy.

questions.question: Which Billboard chart ranks the most popular songs in the United States each week?
questions.explanation: The Billboard Hot 100 is THE chart for singles in the US. It factors in streaming, radio, and sales. Getting a #1 there is like winning the musical World Cup.

questions.question: Put these Beatles albums in the order they were released, from earliest to latest:
options: Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band
questions.explanation: A Hard Day's Night (1964), Sgt. Pepper's (1967), Abbey Road (1969), Let It Be (1970). The Beatles evolved from mop-tops to musical revolutionaries in just a few years!

questions.question: What is the term for a musical composition written for a full orchestra, typically in four movements?
questions.explanation: A symphony is an extended composition for orchestra, usually in four movements. Beethoven wrote 9 of them, and the 9th is still giving people chills!

questions.question: Which Jamaican musician is considered the 'King of Reggae'?
questions.explanation: Bob Marley is reggae royalty. 'One Love', 'No Woman No Cry', 'Redemption Song' — his music is timeless. You'll hear his tunes from Kingston to Camps Bay!

questions.question: What scale uses only the black keys on a piano and is common in East Asian music?
questions.explanation: The pentatonic scale has 5 notes and you can play it on just the black keys. It's used everywhere — from Chinese traditional music to blues and rock!

questions.question: Which music streaming platform, launched in Sweden in 2008, became the world's largest by subscriber count?
questions.explanation: Spotify changed the game. Daniel Ek started it in Stockholm and now it has over 600 million users. RIP to buying CDs at Musica!

questions.question: Miriam Makeba, known as 'Mama Africa', popularised which style of vocal clicking in her international hit?
questions.explanation: Miriam Makeba's 'Pata Pata' and 'The Click Song' (Qongqothwane) introduced the world to Xhosa click consonants. She put Mzansi on the global music map!

questions.question: In a standard orchestra, which section typically sits at the back and includes timpani, snare drum, and cymbals?
questions.explanation: Percussion sits at the back of the orchestra. They might look like they're chilling back there, but when those timpani hit, everyone feels it!

questions.question: Arrange these music formats in the order they became mainstream, from earliest to latest:
questions.explanation: Vinyl records (1950s mainstream), CDs (1980s), MP3s (late 1990s/2000s), streaming (2010s). From spinning vinyl at a jol to streaming on the couch — what a journey!

questions.question: What is the musical term for gradually getting louder?
questions.explanation: Crescendo means gradually getting louder. It's Italian, like most music terms. Think of it like the braai getting busier as the afternoon goes on!

questions.question: Which Celia Cruz catchphrase became synonymous with salsa music worldwide?
questions.explanation: Celia Cruz was the 'Queen of Salsa' and her signature shout '¡Azucar!' (meaning 'sugar!') became iconic. She brought Cuban music to the whole world!

questions.question: What rare vocal technique, used by Tuvan throat singers, allows a person to produce two pitches simultaneously?
questions.explanation: Overtone singing (or throat singing) from Tuva lets singers produce a fundamental note and an overtone at the same time. It sounds otherworldly — proper mind-blowing stuff!

questions.question: In twelve-tone serialism, pioneered by Arnold Schoenberg, the composer must use all how many chromatic pitches before repeating any?
questions.explanation: Twelve-tone technique uses all 12 chromatic pitches in a specific order (a 'tone row') before any can repeat. Schoenberg basically said 'rules are boring' and made new ones!

questions.question: Put these classical music periods in chronological order, from earliest to latest:
questions.explanation: Baroque (1600-1750, Bach/Vivaldi), Classical (1750-1820, Mozart/Haydn), Romantic (1820-1900, Chopin/Tchaikovsky), 20th Century Modern (Stravinsky/Schoenberg). Each era pushed boundaries further!

questions.question: The 'Devil's Interval', historically avoided in medieval church music, is technically known as what?
questions.explanation: The tritone (augmented fourth / diminished fifth) was called 'Diabolus in Musica'. Medieval monks thought it sounded evil. Black Sabbath and heavy metal later said 'perfect, we'll take it!'

questions.question: Which 20th-century composer wrote '4′33″', a piece consisting of four minutes and thirty-three seconds of silence?
questions.explanation: John Cage's '4′33″' (1952) is literally silence — the 'music' is whatever ambient sounds occur. It's either genius or the biggest troll in music history. Maybe both!
